title: Customers With Equal Number Of Orders
description: Find customers who have placed the same number of orders as at least one other customer. Return the customer id.
sql_solution: WITH counts AS (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) cnt FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT customer_id FROM counts WHERE cnt IN (SELECT cnt FROM counts GROUP BY cnt HAVING COUNT(*) > 1);
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

customer_counts = orders.groupBy("customer_id").agg(count(lit(1)).alias("cnt"))

duplicate_counts = (
    customer_counts.groupBy("cnt")
    .agg(count(lit(1)).alias("customer_count"))
    .filter(col("customer_count") > 1)
    .select("cnt")
)

result = (
    customer_counts.alias("cc")
    .join(duplicate_counts.alias("dc"), col("cc.cnt") == col("dc.cnt"), "inner")
    .select(col("cc.customer_id").alias("customer_id"))
)
